methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Velo3D is a public-stage additive manufacturing and robotics company that has raised over $300M in total capital. The valuation universe comprises 11 peers within Robotics & Manufacturing, including 1 public comparable (ticker 277810.KQ) and 8 private firms such as 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this analysis. The Robotics & Manufacturing sector contains only 11 same-sector peers overall, and application of stage-filtering (same-stage, adjacent-stage) and valuation-tier filters yields fewer than three datapoints, falling short of the minimum threshold required for reliable median-based triangulation. Drawing a median from the full sector universe without tighter stage or financial profile alignment would introduce substantial survivorship and heterogeneity bias, obscuring rather than clarifying fair value.

Given the limited narrow peer set and inapplicability of quantitative peer triangulation, a defensible valuation range cannot be derived from the data provided. Resolution would require either expansion of the comparison universe to adjacent robotics subsectors, application of DCF or revenue multiple methodologies using Velo3D's operational financials, or identification of additional same-stage or similar-profile public comps. The single public peer in the dataset is insufficient to establish a standalone benchmark. Further analysis and data collection are necessary before a point estimate or range can be responsibly supported.
